transfer_deferred_entry_options: Transfer possible from Danish unis if passed 1st year equiv, empty places; no direct int'l transfer - apply ordinary + credit transfer; deferred not mentioned
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Varies; competitive programs have GPA cutoffs >10/12 Danish scale; IT/eng increasing apps
acceptance_rate_by_residency: No split data; non-EU paying applicants assessed same but fees apply
admitted_gpa_percentiles: No percentiles; min avg grade per program in key stats (e.g. competitive ~10+/12)

scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Danish state scholarships for non-EU masters: automatic with admission app by 15 Jan/15 Sep; merit-based, limited per program (1-2); tuition waiver +/- stipend

average_aid_merit_statistics: Limited scholarships; no avg stats published

name: Adelaide University

application_pathways_eligibility: Domestic Year 12 (ATAR/Selection Rank via SATAC); prior higher ed (GPA); VET; work/life experience; international qualifications (country-specific, foundation pathways). Recent secondary: ATAR + adjustments.
deadline_calendar: No ED/EA/RD; rolling admissions recommended 6 weeks prior. Specific: Medicine/Dentistry/Oral Health Semester 1 2025: June/Sept 2024; others case-by-case.
program_supplemental_requirements: Yes; e.g. UCAT for medicine, auditions/portfolios for arts/music, interviews, math prerequisites for engineering/science.
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Deferral up to 2 years via SATAC; credit/advanced standing for prior study assessed on application.
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Not published; competitiveness via ATAR cutoffs (e.g. 95+ for advanced programs, med ~99).
acceptance_rate_by_residency: Not published; domestic CSP quotas competitive by selection rank; international direct/rolling.
admitted_gpa_percentiles: Not published for undergrad; postgrad entry GPA varies by program (e.g. credit average min).
need_policy: N/A; Australian system: domestic CSP need-based limited, international full-fee paying.
class_profile_composition: Sem1 2024: Recent secondary 51.1%, prior HE 27.9%, VET 1.7%, work exp 2.5%, int'l 16.8%.
selectivity_trend: Not explicitly published; ATAR cutoffs stable/high for competitive programs.
tuition_fees: Domestic CSP ~AUD 8-15k/yr; Int'l indicative AUD 25k-62k/yr depending on program (2025 rates, annual increase).
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Adelaide Academic Excellence 50%: GPA 6.7/7 or ATAR 99; app deadlines Sem1 30 Nov 2025, Sem2 22 May 2026. Global Citizens 15-30%. High achievers.
major_impaction_signals: Quotas on CSP places; competitive selection ranks; some programs subject-based entry if below cutoff.
average_aid_merit_statistics: Not published; merit scholarships 15-50% tuition reduction for high GPA/ATAR.
deferral_waitlist_indicators: Deferral allowed up to 2 years; no waitlist mentioned.

program_supplemental_requirements: Specialized subject tests and entrance exam programs per faculty/program; creative exams for some specialties.
admitted_sat_act_percentiles: SAT/ACT accepted for paid admission with score transfer; no percentiles published

scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Educational grants issued by Ministry of Education; same application deadlines as admission

tuition_fees: €900-€4,500/year based on ISEE; first installment ~€157 fixed; intl similar with docs.
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: ISEE by Nov; International Talents @Unibo (SAT/GRE/merit/need); ER.GO benefits; deadlines vary Oct-Mar.

english_test_requirements: TOEFL iBT (min 100, subscores 22+), IELTS, DET (120+), or Cambridge (185+) recommended if primary language not English or scores below SAT ERW 690/ACT 30 Reading/English.
application_pathways_eligibility: ED I, ED II, RD for first-year; Transfer (domestic need-blind); QuestBridge partner; International eligible but need-aware if seeking aid; DACA/undocumented eligible.
deadline_calendar: ED I: Nov 1, 2025; ED II: Jan 2, 2026; RD: Jan 2, 2026; Transfer: Mar 1, 2026. Aid deadlines 15 days after app.
program_supplemental_requirements: Hopkins supplemental essay required via Common App or Coalition.
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Transfer applications due Mar 1; need-blind for domestic transfers. Deferred entry not explicitly detailed, case-by-case assumed.
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Not publicly available; overall rate applies to KSAS and WSE.
acceptance_rate_by_residency: Private institution, no in-state preference. International ~4.5% (prior class); enrolled int'l ~16%.
admitted_gpa_percentiles: Average unweighted GPA 3.93. Breakdown: 4.0 (65%), 3.75-3.99 (27%), 3.50-3.74 (5%), lower <3% (fall 2024).
need_policy: Need-blind for U.S. citizens/PR/eligible non-citizens/DACA/undocumented; need-aware for international students applying for aid. Meets 100% demonstrated need.
class_profile_composition: ~5,600 undergrads; 45% male/55% female; 30% FLI; int'l from 100+ countries (~16% first-year); 21% first-gen (Class 2029).
selectivity_trend: Stable low single digits: 7.5% (2024), 6.4% (2025), ~6% recent years; apps up to 49k.
tuition_fees: $66,670 tuition (2025-26); total COA ~$91,830 on-campus.
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Need-based scholarships automatic with aid app (deadlines Nov15/Jan15); merit limited (e.g. Westgate Engineering); QuestBridge; Cummings/DC/Baltimore publics.
acceptance_rate_by_round: ED ~13-14%, RD ~4-6% (prior cycles, e.g. Class 2028 ED 13.6%, RD 6.4%).
major_impaction_signals: No explicit impaction; popular: bio/engineering (~50% intend).
average_aid_merit_statistics: Avg need-based scholarship $63k-$66k first-year; 52% receive need-based aid.
deferral_waitlist_indicators: No specific data available.

program_specific_acceptance_rate: Varies; higher weighted % for competitive like medicine/engineering (80-90%+)
acceptance_rate_by_residency: Saudis prioritized; non-Saudis separate quotas (10-30%)
admitted_gpa_percentiles: No US GPA; admitted weighted percentages 70-95%+
admitted_sat_act_percentiles: SAT/ACT not used for Saudis; GAT/AT required; internationals min SAT 1000
need_policy: N/A; public, free for Saudis; merit-based scholarships

tuition_fees: Free for Saudis; non-Saudis 15,000-30,000 SAR/year bachelor's

name: King Fahd University of Petroleum & Minerals
test_policy: Required: General Aptitude Test (Qudrat) ≥85 (Primary track), KFUPM Achievement Test (Primary), SAT ≥1350 (SAT track), no aptitude/achievement for SAT/Olympiad.
english_test_requirements: Primary track: STEP ≥65, IELTS ≥4.5, TOEFL iBT ≥45, Duolingo ≥80; Regular: STEP ≥55 or equiv.
application_pathways_eligibility: Tracks: Primary (Qudrat+Tahsili+HS), SAT (SAT+math test), Olympiad (winners), Secondary (vacancies). Recent HS grads (nat sci); Saudi/non-Saudi w/Saudi mother; internationals via SAT/ISEF.
deadline_calendar: SAT Phase1: Jan 5-14 2025? Wait 2026 dates: SAT Feb1-15 2026, Primary Jan15-29 2026, Olympiad Apr1-Aug1 2025 (update for 2026), Secondary Jun15-24 2025.
program_supplemental_requirements: SOP, recommendation letter (most tracks), university math test (SAT), possible interview; major choice post-admit.
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Transfer from other universities or DCC available; no deferred entry explicitly mentioned.
admitted_sat_act_percentiles: SAT min 1350 for eligibility (SAT track), no percentiles.
need_policy: Merit-based full scholarships standard; additional need-based for internationals.
tuition_fees: Saudis: Free via govt scholarship; Non-Saudis: SR 20,000/year (waived by full scholarship).
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Full scholarship with admission (merit); additional need/merit post-admit, deadlines announced.
major_impaction_signals: Seats limited per track/major; competitive admission.
average_aid_merit_statistics: Full tuition waiver + stipend/housing for scholarship recipients (most admits).

name: King's College London
overall_acceptance_rate: UCAS undergraduate offer rate approximately 43% (2024 cycle); enrollment/acceptance rate ~14%. Varies significantly by faculty (e.g., Business 19%, Arts & Humanities 75%). Detailed stats in faculty PDFs.
test_policy: Test-blind for standard qualifications (A-levels/IB). Standardized tests (SAT/ACT) accepted but not required for US high school applicants. Program-specific tests required (e.g., LNAT for Law by Dec 31, UCAT for Medicine/Dentistry).
english_test_requirements: IELTS Academic 6.5 overall (min 6.0 each skill) for most UG (Band B); higher for competitive programs (Band A: 7.5/7.0). TOEFL iBT 92 (min 23 writing, 20 others). PTE 62/59. Valid within 2 years. Exemptions for majority English-speaking countries or certain quals.
application_pathways_eligibility: All undergraduate applications via UCAS. Open to UK, EU, international students. International Foundation Programme for progression to UG. Study Abroad options. Graduates considered with 2:1 degree. Contextual offers for eligible UK applicants.
deadline_calendar: No ED/EA. Medicine/Dentistry (exc. some): 15 Oct prior year. All other UG: 26 Jan (equal consideration). Late apps possible via UCAS Clearing.
program_supplemental_requirements: Personal statement and reference via UCAS. Program-specific: LNAT (Law), UCAT/UCAT (Med/Dent), subject requirements (e.g., A in English Lit for English BA). Work experience not required but beneficial for some.
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Transfers not permitted for most programs (e.g., History, Law). Deferred entry possible via UCAS (select on application).
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Varies by faculty (2024): Arts & Humanities offer 75%, acceptance 21%; Business 19%/5%; Dentistry 13%/4%; NMES 60%/22%; etc. See PDFs for details.
acceptance_rate_by_residency: Stats split UK/non-UK in official PDFs (e.g., China separate). No specific rates published; international competitive.
admitted_sat_act_percentiles: SAT/ACT considered for US applicants but no percentiles published.
need_policy: Need-aware for international students (high fees, limited scholarships). Home students: government loans/grants.
class_profile_composition: 64% female, 35% male; 61% UK, 31% international, 8% EU; 82% full-time UG.
selectivity_trend: Stable; offer rates vary but competitive. Detailed in 5-year faculty PDFs.
tuition_fees: Home: £9,535/year (2025/26). International: Varies by course/programme, typically £25,000-£40,000/year (check individual course pages).
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Various: e.g., Vice-Chancellor’s Awards £5k for Indian PG (apply by 31 May 2026); King's Scholars of Tomorrow £5k for Foundation (15 May 2025); Progression scholarship 10% first-year fee.
average_aid_merit_statistics: Limited merit scholarships; average not published.
yield_rate: ~14% overall enrollment rate from applications (2024); varies (e.g., 21% Arts, 5% Business).
deferral_waitlist_indicators: Deferred entry via UCAS possible. No waitlist mentioned.

english_test_requirements: Required for English-track programs (TOEFL iBT, IELTS Academic, Duolingo, PTE, etc.); exemptions for native speakers or English-medium education

program_supplemental_requirements: English proficiency mandatory for International Studies (English track); optional portfolios/tests for some arts/engineering.
acceptance_rate_by_residency: Separate tracks: Korean CSAT vs International quota (limited to 10%)

class_profile_composition: Enrollment ~24,000 undergrad; intl students increasing
tuition_fees: Per semester 2026: Humanities/Social ~5.8M KRW (~$4K USD); Engineering ~7.7M KRW (~$5.2K); varies by college
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Available via KU scholarship program; requires English proficiency even for exempt; details in app guide
major_impaction_signals: Intl quota <=10% total; some majors English req

name: Massey University
test_policy: Not required; no mention of SAT/ACT or standardized tests in entry requirements.
english_test_requirements: Undergrad: IELTS 6.0 (no band <5.5), TOEFL iBT 80 (writing 19+), PTE 50 (no comm skill <42), equivalents for Cambridge, NZCEL, etc. Higher for some programs/postgrad.
application_pathways_eligibility: Domestic NZ/Aus: NCEA L3/equiv (ATAR 74+), adult entry (20+ no UE needed), discretionary entrance for school leavers/home-schooled without UE. International: country-specific high school equivs. Preferential entry score for oversubscribed progs.
deadline_calendar: No ED/EA/RD; Semester 1 2026: Intl new 31 Oct 2025, Dom 5 Feb 2026. Sem 2: Intl 1 May 2026, Dom 19 Jun 2026. Selected-entry earlier (e.g. Nursing S1 31 Jan 2026). Applications after if spaces avail.
program_supplemental_requirements: Selected-entry qualifications require extra forms/info (e.g. personal statements, refs for Nursing, Aviation). Download forms from site.
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Recognition of prior learning (RPL) for credits from previous study/work experience. Deferral/withdrawal options available; contact for specifics.

major_impaction_signals: Preferential Entry Score ranks applicants for oversubscribed undergrad quals; higher scores admitted first until places filled.
deferral_waitlist_indicators: Course waiting lists with special permission option. Admissions continue post-deadline if spaces; no explicit deferral stats.

name: McGill University
overall_acceptance_rate: 47.5% offers rate (18,132 offers / 38,135 applicants) and 19.2% enrollment rate (7,310 entering) for Fall 2025 undergraduate
test_policy: Test-optional: ACT/SAT scores not required for Fall 2026 applicants to most undergraduate programs
english_test_requirements: Required unless exempt (e.g. 4+ years English-medium schooling). TOEFL iBT 100+ (no subscore <22, except B.Ed. TESL/B.Com 130+); IELTS 6.5+ (no band <6.0); etc. Official scores direct to McGill.
application_pathways_eligibility: Separate requirements by HS type: US (Top 5 GPA or % avg), Canadian (outside QC/ON: Top 5 Gr12), International (IB 30-37, etc.), University transfer (space avail, min CGPA 3.0+). Quebec CEGEP separate.
deadline_calendar: No ED/EA; RD: App Jan 15 (intl/Can/US), QC later; Docs Mar 1; MES app Jan21-Feb7-Mar7 by region; Music/Social Work earlier. Varies by program.
program_supplemental_requirements: Music: Audition/portfolio; Nursing: French proficiency; Education/TESL: Language tests; Architecture: Portfolio; etc. See program pages.
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Transfers: Space-available, min CGPA 3.0-3.4 past 3 yrs varies by program; 60 credits residency req. Deferral: Possible upon request to admissions.
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Enrollment rates (entering/app): Engineering 12.6% (1028/8188), Arts 20.3% (2204/10845), Management 10.1% (736/7278), etc. for Fall 2025. No offer rates per program.
acceptance_rate_by_residency: No direct rates; Entering class composition Fall 2025: Quebec 48.8%, Rest Canada 22.4%, US 13.7%, Overseas 15.2%.
admitted_gpa_percentiles: No percentiles published. Minimums/cutoffs vary: e.g. 90-95% Top 5 for competitive programs.
need_policy: Need-aware: Need-based aid available but admissions holistic incl. finances implicitly as public uni.
class_profile_composition: Fall 2025 entering undergrad: Quebec 48.8%, ROC 22.4%, US 13.7%, Intl 15.2%; By faculty e.g. Eng 1028, Arts 2204.

scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Major Entrance Scholarships: Separate app; Deadlines: QC CEGEP Mar7, Can Feb7, Intl Jan21. Auto consider 1-yr scholarships on academics. Need-based bursaries separate.
acceptance_rate_by_round: No ED/EA rounds; Single RD deadline primarily.
major_impaction_signals: High-demand programs (Eng, Mgmt) lower enrollment rates; Transfers space-limited.
average_aid_merit_statistics: $7.1M need-based bursaries to 619 students (avg ~$11.5k); $7M+ merit to 2200+ (last cycle).

name: The University of New South Wales (UNSW Sydney)
test_policy: Test-blind for SAT/ACT; no standardized tests like SAT/ACT required
english_test_requirements: IELTS Academic 6.5 overall (no band <6.0); TOEFL iBT 90 (writing 23); PTE 64 (no <54); tests <2 years old
application_pathways_eligibility: Domestic: UAC (ATAR-based); International: direct apply or agent, foundation/diploma pathways, prior study; Recent school leavers ATAR, non-recent prior quals
deadline_calendar: No ED/EA; UAC rounds: Dec Round 2 offers ~23 Dec 2025 for T1 2026; Jan Round 1 ~8 Jan 2026; Intl grouped offer rounds by term (T1 Feb, T2 May/Jun, T3 Sep)
program_supplemental_requirements: Assumed knowledge (e.g. Maths); Portfolios for Art/Design/Built Env; Auditions Music; Interviews some programs
transfer_deferred_entry_options: University transfer via prior study assessment; SCATS for circumstances; Deferrals no longer auto-approved, case-by-case; Non-school leavers pathways
need_policy: Need-aware; admissions merit-based, financial circumstances via scholarships/EAS not affecting admission
tuition_fees: Domestic CSP: Band 1 $4,627-$9,314 EFTSL; Intl ~A$45,000-$55,000/yr (indicative, program-specific)
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Merit-based e.g. International Scientia; apply via UNSW portal, deadlines align with offers/UAC
major_impaction_signals: Some programs exhausted NOSC for 2026 intl; high-demand fill early e.g. Commerce
deferral_waitlist_indicators: Deferrals no longer automatically approved; must contact admissions; no waitlists mentioned

name: The University of Sheffield
overall_acceptance_rate: Approximately 13% (domestic); ~85% (international)
test_policy: Not applicable; no SAT/ACT required. Admissions based on academic qualifications (A-levels, IB etc.)
english_test_requirements: IELTS 6.0 overall (min 5.5 each) typical for undergrad; varies by course. Alternatives: TOEFL, PTE etc. GCSE Eng 4/C for UK.
application_pathways_eligibility: All undergrad via UCAS. Open to UK/EU/International. Foundation/International College for some.
deadline_calendar: UCAS: 2 Sep 2025-14 Jan 2026 equal consideration; 15 Jan-30 Jun 2026 late (except Med/Dent 15 Oct 2025)
program_supplemental_requirements: Varies; e.g. Medicine: UCAT/BMAT, interviews, work experience. Check online prospectus.
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Deferred entry accepted. Transfers to Year 2 possible if equivalent study completed.
acceptance_rate_by_residency: Domestic ~13%; International ~85%
need_policy: Need-aware; means-tested bursaries for home students (£250-£1000 based on income ≤£40k)
class_profile_composition: 64% UK, 33% International, 3% EU; 53% female, 47% male; 92% full-time
tuition_fees: Home: £9,790/year (2026); Overseas: £23,810-£30,570/year (2025/26); Med/Dent higher (£45k+)
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Int'l Undergrad Scholarship £2,500/yr (60% avg), firm/insure by 3 Jun 2026. Access Sheffield for home ≤£40k income, apply by 3 Jun 2026.
deferral_waitlist_indicators: Deferrals accepted; no waitlists (UCAS system uses offers/declines)

name: The University of Sydney
test_policy: Optional (SAT/ACT recognized for qualification equivalence but not required; do not satisfy math prereqs)
english_test_requirements: IELTS Academic 6.5 overall (no band <6.0); TOEFL iBT 85+; PTE 61+; tests <2 years old at commencement; course-specific variations
application_pathways_eligibility: Domestic: UAC (Year 12, IB in Australia, NCEA); International: Direct to Uni or UAC for specific (Aus Year12/IB outside); transfers after 1yr uni
deadline_calendar: No ED/EA; Semester 1 (Feb): Int'l app close 1 Dec prior, Dom UAC dates; Sem 2 (Aug): Int'l 15 May, Dom 15 Jul
program_supplemental_requirements: Some courses require interview, portfolio, audition, questionnaire; listed on course pages
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Transfers: Eligible after 1yr full-time uni (assessed on uni GPA or ATAR); credit via Go8 agreement; Deferral: Possible, check course page
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Varies by course; e.g. via lowest/highest selection ranks (ATAR equiv) published for 2025 admits
acceptance_rate_by_residency: Not published; separate processes for domestic/int'l
admitted_gpa_percentiles: ATAR-based; e.g. program lowest/highest selection ranks 75-99+; no GPA percentiles
admitted_sat_act_percentiles: Not applicable/required; SAT/ACT recognized for quals but no percentiles published
need_policy: Need-aware (merit-based aid; no need-blind admissions)
class_profile_composition: Undergrad: 38% int'l, 55% female, 29% alt pathways, equity groups (low-SES 2.9k etc.)
selectivity_trend: Stable/increasing competitiveness; ATAR cutoffs steady, more alt entry
tuition_fees: Domestic: CSP ~AUD 4k-11k/yr; Int'l: AUD 45k-60k/yr (indicative 2026)
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Merit-based; separate apps/deadlines; e.g. Vice-Chancellor's Int'l ~95 ATAR equiv
major_impaction_signals: High-demand courses limited offers pre-Dec Round 2; ATAR varies
average_aid_merit_statistics: Not published
deferral_waitlist_indicators: Deferral possible; no waitlist mentioned

name: The University of Tokyo
overall_acceptance_rate: Approximately 32% (3,084 successful applicants out of 9,688 total applicants for undergraduate programs, as of May 1, 2024). Primarily for domestic students via national entrance exam.
test_policy: Required: National university entrance examination for traditional Japanese-taught undergraduate programs. For PEAK English programs (international): Standardized tests (e.g., SAT, ACT, IB, A-levels) are accepted and considered in document review; not strictly required but strongly recommended.
english_test_requirements: Not explicitly required for PEAK (English programs); English proficiency demonstrated through education in English-medium schools or standardized tests. TOEFL/IELTS mentioned in secondary sources.
application_pathways_eligibility: Traditional programs (Japanese): Domestic via national exam + UTokyo exam; high Japanese proficiency required for internationals. PEAK (English, international-focused): 12 years schooling, not Japanese national or recent resident in Japan, expected graduation by Sep 2026. No dual Japanese citizenship.
deadline_calendar: PEAK Sep 2026: Application Nov 11-Dec 9 2025; results Mar 30 2026; enrollment Sep 1 2026. Traditional: Varies, generally ~9 months prior. No ED/EA; single round.
program_supplemental_requirements: PEAK JEA: Essays on Japan/East Asia. ES: Math test (online pre-interview), essays on environment/science. Both: Interviews, standardized test scores if applicable.
transfer_deferred_entry_options: No mention of transfer or deferred entry for undergraduates. Exchange programs (USTEP) available but not degree transfer.
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Not publicly released for PEAK or specific programs.
acceptance_rate_by_residency: Not available; traditional programs primarily domestic Japanese.
admitted_gpa_percentiles: Not reported; holistic review for PEAK.
need_policy: Need-aware; public university with limited aid.
class_profile_composition: Enrollment 28,479 total; undergrad ~14,000. ~13% international (mostly grad). No detailed undergrad demo.
tuition_fees: Undergraduate annual: ¥642,960 (approx. $4,300 USD); Admission fee: ¥282,000. Same for international/domestic.
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Merit-based scholarships offered at PEAK admission (Mar 2026). Tuition exemptions for economic need, apply post-enrollment. MEXT pre-arrival.
acceptance_rate_by_round: No early decision/early action; single application round.
major_impaction_signals: No major-specific impaction; program choice at application (JEA/ES for PEAK).
average_aid_merit_statistics: Not available.
deferral_waitlist_indicators: No information on deferrals or waitlists.

name: Tongji University
test_policy: Required: China Scholastic Competency Assessment (CSCA) for all international undergraduate applicants. HSK for Chinese-taught (e.g. New HSK Level 5, 180+ points). No SAT/ACT mentioned.
english_test_requirements: For English-taught programs: IELTS 6.5 or TOEFL iBT 85+. Native English speakers exempt. Not required for Chinese-taught.
application_pathways_eligibility: International students (non-Chinese citizens, high school graduates or above, aged 18-25 for scholarships/18-35 self-funded, good health). Special rules for applicants from Mainland China/HK/Macao/Taiwan immigrants (4+ years passport, 2 years overseas residence). Online application via study-info.tongji.edu.cn.
deadline_calendar: Undergraduate: January 20, 2026 - March 31, 2026. No ED/EA/RD; single application period. Scholarships may have earlier country-specific deadlines.
program_supplemental_requirements: Architecture/Urban Planning/Landscape: 3 paintings + 1 freehand perspective. Other programs may require additional materials.
need_policy: Not applicable; financial need not primary factor. Scholarships merit-based.
class_profile_composition: Total students ~37,000; intl students 3,468 (1,622 degree-seeking) from ~100 countries (2019 data). No detailed demographics.
tuition_fees: Undergraduate intl: RMB 20,000-45,000/year depending on program (e.g. Architecture 33,800 RMB, Medicine 45,000 RMB).
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Chinese Govt Scholarship: country-specific Nov-Mar. Shanghai Govt: earlier periods. Tongji Excellence: English-taught undergrad. Merit-based, age/health/academic reqs.

name: Trinity College Dublin
overall_acceptance_rate: No official overall rate; CAO EU ~17% (4059 round 1 offers from 23548 applicants 2025); international ~33% secondary
test_policy: Test-optional: SAT/ACT not required, considered if submitted for US applicants
english_test_requirements: IELTS 6.5-7.0 (band dependent); TOEFL iBT 88-110; Duolingo 120+; full bands on site
application_pathways_eligibility: EU/Irish via CAO; non-EU direct application; residency determines fee status
deadline_calendar: Non-EU UG: priority Feb 1, final June 30; CAO standard Feb 1 early, May change of mind; rolling after May
program_supplemental_requirements: Some courses: interviews, portfolios, tests (e.g. music); check course pages
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Transfers and re-admissions possible via separate applications; deferred entry info available
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Varies by CAO points (e.g. Medicine 600+, Dental 625); no rates published
acceptance_rate_by_residency: EU/CAO more competitive (~13-17%); non-EU higher (~33-50% secondary)
admitted_gpa_percentiles: US min GPA 3.2 unweighted; no percentiles published
need_policy: No info; likely need-aware for non-EU full-pay
class_profile_composition: No published profile
selectivity_trend: Applications up: 2026 +6.2% first pref, 2025 +13.5%; offers record 4059 (2025)
tuition_fees: EU: ~€5,681-€7,332/yr (tuition+contrib); non-EU: €22,580 Arts, €28,900+ STEM 2025/26
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Global Excellence: up to €5k non-EU yr1, rolling/region-specific (Apr-Jun); country-specific e.g. China Claddagh
acceptance_rate_by_round: No ED/EA; CAO rounds, round 1 most competitive
major_impaction_signals: High-demand: Medicine, Dental, PPES 600+ points; random selection for some
average_aid_merit_statistics: No stats; scholarships €2-5k reductions
deferral_waitlist_indicators: No info

name: Tsinghua University
overall_acceptance_rate: Estimated 15-30% for international undergraduates; <0.3% for domestic via Gaokao
test_policy: Optional: SAT/ACT/AP/IB/A-Level etc. as supporting materials for academic performance
english_test_requirements: TOEFL/IELTS required for non-native speakers applying to English-taught programs
application_pathways_eligibility: International applicants only: foreign citizens in good health, high school graduates, no prior Chinese citizenship per MOE Doc No.12 (2020); aged 18+ by Sep 1 2026
deadline_calendar: First Round (Arts/Design only): Sep 30-Nov 28 2025; Second Round: Nov 29 2025-Feb 28 2026; English-taught per program guide
program_supplemental_requirements: Personal statement, 3-min video self-intro, 2 recommendation letters, transcripts, national exam certs, HSK if applicable, financial sponsor form; integrative test for shortlisted
acceptance_rate_by_residency: N/A - no residency-based admissions; separate international track
tuition_fees: 26,000-40,000 RMB/year most programs; 40,000+ Arts/Clinical Medicine; App fee 800 RMB; Accom 40 RMB/day double; Ins 800/year
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Chinese Gov Scholarship (CSC Type A): Nov 2024-Apr 2025 via embassy; Beijing Gov, Tsinghua scholarships: separate apps post-admission; for outstanding freshmen

name: UCL
overall_acceptance_rate: 10.2% (9,138 places from 89,239 applications; 9.8 apps per place)
test_policy: Not required (no SAT/ACT or other standardized tests; assessment based on qualifications like A-levels/IB)
english_test_requirements: IELTS Academic Level 1-5 by program: L1 6.5 (6.0 each), L2 7.0 (6.5), L3 7.0 (7.0), L4 7.5 (7.0), L5 8.0 (8.0). TOEFL iBT: L1 92 (24/20), etc. Valid 2 years prior.
application_pathways_eligibility: All undergraduate applications via UCAS (code U80). Same process for UK/international/mature/disabled. UPC for some international.
deadline_calendar: No ED/EA; Medicine: 15 Oct 2025; Equal consideration: 14 Jan 2026 (18:00 UK); Late/Extra until Jun 2026.
program_supplemental_requirements: Some programs require admissions tests (e.g. TARA, TMUA, ESAT). Personal statement via UCAS; no additional for most.
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Deferred: one year only. Transfers: majority Year 1; limited Year 2 for specific programs (e.g. some engineering/management).
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Varies: e.g. Computer Science 17.6 apps/place, Law 22.8, Economics 9.3, average 9.8.
acceptance_rate_by_residency: No direct rates; enrolled: 46% UK-domiciled, 46% international (HESA).
admitted_gpa_percentiles: N/A (uses A-levels A*A*A-ABB or IB 36-40; no GPA reported)
need_policy: Need-aware for international; UK domestic eligible for gov loans regardless of need.
class_profile_composition: 61% female, 38% male, 46% UK, 46% international, 8% EU (HESA undergrad).
tuition_fees: UK: £9,790/yr; Overseas: £29,800-£39,800/yr (program-specific, cohort guarantee).
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Program-specific scholarships; check prospectus. Deadlines vary, some UCAS-tied.
major_impaction_signals: High apps/place: Management 34.7, Comp Sci 17.6, Law 22.8.
average_aid_merit_statistics: Limited data; various scholarships available but averages not published.
deferral_waitlist_indicators: Deferral: one year max; no waitlist mentioned.

name: United Arab Emirates University
overall_acceptance_rate: Estimated 34%
test_policy: English proficiency test required (TOEFL/IELTS or HS English 80%); SAT/ACT not required but equivalents accepted for math/science
english_test_requirements: HS English 80% or TOEFL ITP 500, iBT 61, IELTS 5.0; higher for some programs (e.g. Medicine 5.5 IELTS)
application_pathways_eligibility: P1: Current HS grads UAE nationals/children of mothers via specific portal; P2/P3: Non-current nationals; Transfers from federal inst; Internationals/children of staff via admission system; Medicine/Vet restricted to nationals
deadline_calendar: Fall: May-July; Spring: Sep-Nov; No ED/EA/RD mentioned
program_supplemental_requirements: College-specific min HS % in subjects/math/science/English; e.g. Medicine: HS 90% English/Math/Science; EmSAT equivalents; Arabic for some
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Transfers from other institutions reviewed case-by-case; Deferral/postponement up to 2 semesters allowed per policy
acceptance_rate_by_residency: Priority admission and scholarships to P1 UAE nationals/children of mothers; Internationals limited and competitive
admitted_sat_act_percentiles: SAT Math equivalents used (e.g. 520+ for some); no percentiles or requirement
need_policy: Not specified; public uni prioritizes merit/nationality
class_profile_composition: Spring 2025: 17,722 total students (16,240 undergrad), 71% female, 29% male; distributed across colleges
tuition_fees: Nationals: discounts/scholarships; Internationals: e.g. ~AED 2,500 (unclear per credit/semester?); application fee required
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Merit-based (100%/75%/50% tuition discount) for high HS % (e.g. 98%+ for elite); same app deadlines May-Jul/Sep-Nov; GPA maintenance 3.6-3.8; internationals eligible
major_impaction_signals: Competitive admission based on capacity per college; Medicine/Vet restricted
deferral_waitlist_indicators: Postponement up to 2 semesters; no waitlist mentioned

name: Universidad de Buenos Aires
overall_acceptance_rate: Open enrollment (effectively 100%; irrestricto, no entrance exam)
test_policy: No standardized tests required
english_test_requirements: None required for undergraduate admission; Spanish proficiency (C1) may be needed for non-native speakers in some cases
application_pathways_eligibility: Open to all high school graduates (Argentines with DNI; foreigners with legalized secondary title and DNI/provisional); all via CBC; exchange/visiting separate
deadline_calendar: CBC main: Feb 2-18 2026; late: Mar 6-11 2026; no ED/EA/RD
program_supplemental_requirements: None for CBC entry; common 6 subjects (2 orientation, 2 career-specific, 2 common); post-CBC faculty progression
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Transfers via equivalency recognition post-CBC; no formal deferred entry; re-inscription possible
need_policy: Need-blind (tuition-free public university; open admission)
class_profile_composition: ~263,000 total enrollment; primarily Argentine residents; ~15,000 international (older data)
tuition_fees: Free for all undergraduate students (Argentines and internationals)
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Sarmiento aid: apply Sep 2025, low-income CBC/degree students up to age 30; Progresar via national; economic vulnerability focus

name: Universiti Putra Malaysia (UPM)
overall_acceptance_rate: Approximately 40%
test_policy: Not required
english_test_requirements: IELTS band 5.5+; TOEFL iBT 60+; PTE 43+; Cambridge English (CAE/CPE/Linguaskill) 154+; MUET band 3+; CIEP level 107+
application_pathways_eligibility: Malaysians: Main channel via UPUOnline (Ministry system); Inclusive channel direct to UPM for Foundation and 81 Bachelor programs (full fee). Internationals: Online application via UPM portal.
deadline_calendar: No ED/EA; International September intake deadline approx. July 15; February intake approx. Nov 30 (varies by year)
program_supplemental_requirements: Faculty-specific minimum grades e.g. C in relevant STPM subjects like Math, Biology, Chemistry; interviews for some programs
admitted_gpa_percentiles: Minimum CGPA 2.00 (Matriculation/Foundation); STPM Grade C (GP 2.00)
tuition_fees: International undergrad: MYR 8,000-10,000 per semester (e.g. Agriculture MYR 10,000/sem, Economics MYR 8,000/sem); Local subsidized much lower via UPU
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Limited undergrad info; e.g. MIS for postgrad (May deadline); PASS for alumni (Jan)

name: Universiti Teknologi Malaysia
overall_acceptance_rate: No official data published; third-party estimates 60-63%
test_policy: No SAT/ACT required; admission based on national high school qualifications (e.g. STPM for Malaysians, equivalent high school certs for international)
english_test_requirements: International students must pass English proficiency test (IELTS/TOEFL/TOEFL iBT/others); Malaysian students require MUET Band 1.0+
application_pathways_eligibility: Malaysian: SPM/STPM/Matriculation/Diploma via UPUOnline or UTM SMARt; International: Senior High School Certificate or equivalent with specific subject scores (e.g. 80% in Math/Physics for Engineering)
deadline_calendar: Feb 2026 intake: Malaysian UG Nov 1 - Dec 31 2025; International UG Nov 1 2025 - Jan 9 2026; No ED/EA rounds
program_supplemental_requirements: UTM interview required for Bachelor of Architecture and Bachelor of Science Industrial Design
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Transfer credits available after faculty registration; Deferred entry not mentioned
tuition_fees: International UG: Engineering RM 75,000 total (USD 17,700); Computer/IT RM 71,000; Management RM 63,000; excludes accommodation (rates subject to change)
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: UTM Zamalah (PhD Malaysian): monthly allowance RM1,500+, sem 1-3; Int'l Doctoral Fellowship: RM2,000 waiver, sem 1-6; applications by semester, GPA/CGPA reqs, no other aid

name: University College Cork
overall_acceptance_rate: No official overall acceptance rate published; admissions via CAO points per program (e.g., CK701 Medicine Round 1 730/625 pts 2025).
test_policy: Not required; standardized tests like SAT/ACT considered optionally for non-EU (e.g., US) applicants alongside GPA.
english_test_requirements: IELTS 6.5 overall (min 6.0/band) CACSSS/COBL/SEFS; 6.5 all bands CoMH (exc Speech Therapy); TOEFL iBT equiv (90 overall pre-2026); Duolingo DET 120 (110/sub); 2yr validity; exemptions for English-medium prior qual/majority English countries.
application_pathways_eligibility: EU/Irish/EFTA/UK: CAO (school leavers, mature >=23yrs, HEAR/DARE access, QQI FET); Non-EU/International: direct UCCApply; min age 17 by Jan post-entry (exemptions possible); Medicine add HPAT/GAMSAT.
deadline_calendar: CAO main: 1 Feb; late 6 Mar-1 May (no restricted/mature); Change of Mind May-Jul; Offers: Round 0 Aug7, R1 Aug27, R2 Sep8 etc.; No ED/EA.
program_supplemental_requirements: Medicine CK701: HPAT-Ireland; Grad Med CK791: GAMSAT; Music CK104/Theatre CK112: audition/entrance exam; some random selection if ties; Garda vetting for health/social care.
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Deferred entry possible (apply via CAO); Transfers: exemptions for prior study, degree pathway options after year1, QQI/BTEC entry; limited details.
program_specific_acceptance_rate: No rates published; CAO Round1 points 2025 and approx places (e.g., CK701 Med 730pts/120 places; CK101 Arts 300/500).
admitted_gpa_percentiles: For US non-EU: min GPA 3.0-3.7/4.0 by competitiveness band; no percentiles.
admitted_sat_act_percentiles: For US non-EU: SAT 1140-1470 or ACT 23-32 by band; no admitted percentiles.
tuition_fees: EU: Free Fees tuition (eligible) + Student Contribution €3000 + Capitation €210 (total ~€3210-€5000+ prog var 2026/27); Non-EU: €16,300-€25,000/yr (2025-26) + €207 cap; e.g., Arts €18,130.
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Automatic 15% for CACSSS non-EU undergrad; others via program; limited details/deadlines.
major_impaction_signals: High-demand progs: Med 730pts/120pl; Dent 613/26; random tiebreak; 5% HEAR/DARE reserves.
deferral_waitlist_indicators: No waitlists; random score for ties; deferral option available.

name: University of Adelaide
test_policy: Test blind - SAT/ACT not used; ATAR/qualifications basis
english_test_requirements: IELTS 6.5 (no band <6.0), TOEFL iBT 79, PTE Academic 65; program-specific variations
application_pathways_eligibility: Recent secondary (ATAR equiv), prior higher ed, VET, work/life exp, international quals; domestic via SATAC, int'l direct/agent
deadline_calendar: No ED/EA; Sem1 (Feb) most apply 6wks prior; program-specific e.g. Med/Dent 30Jun S1, Veterinary 30Sep
program_supplemental_requirements: Prereqs (e.g. maths/physics), UCAT/interviews/auditions/portfolio for select programs
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Deferral up to 2 years; credit/advanced standing for prior qual
need_policy: Need-aware (merit scholarships only; no need-based aid)
class_profile_composition: Sem1 2024 new students: Recent secondary 51.1%, prior HE 27.9%, int'l 16.8%, VET 1.7%, work exp 2.5%
tuition_fees: Int'l undergrad ~AUD 25k-53k/yr (indicative 2025); varies by program; research 53k-62k
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Global Citizens 15/30% auto-assess on ATAR/GPA (e.g. ATAR85/95, GPA5.5/6.5); no separate deadline
deferral_waitlist_indicators: Deferral allowed up to 2yrs; no waitlist mentioned

name: University of Alberta
test_policy: Not required; SAT/ACT optional for US-patterned curriculum applicants at accredited schools
english_test_requirements: IELTS Academic 6.5 overall (6.0 each band); TOEFL iBT 90 (21 each before 2026, 4.5 after); Duolingo 120; or equivalent high school/prior education
application_pathways_eligibility: High school applicants (Grade 12 courses + competitive average); postsecondary transfer (GPA-based); international (curriculum equivalents + ELP); Indigenous (equity averages); nonmatriculated; etc.
deadline_calendar: No ED/EA; rolling from Oct 1; program-specific e.g. Arts BA Honors Apr 1 2026, Augustana Jun 30 2026, some May 1
program_supplemental_requirements: Varies: auditions (Music), portfolios (Design/Drama), interviews/references (Education/Health Sciences), personal statements
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Transfer applicants assessed on postsecondary GPA/credits; resources available; deferred entry not explicitly detailed
acceptance_rate_by_residency: No rates; higher GPA for out-of-province e.g. Pharmacy 3.5 Alberta vs 3.7 non-Alberta
selectivity_trend: Averages vary yearly by applicant pool/program capacity; 3-year trends shown e.g. stable low-mid 70s for many
tuition_fees: 2025-26 Canadian: ~$7,150 tuition (full year Arts/Science) + ~$3,700 fees/books; $715/3-unit course; international + differential fee (~2x)
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: $52M+ annually; automatic entrance up to $6,000 (top 5% by average); application-based deadline Jan 11
major_impaction_signals: Higher competitive averages signal impaction e.g. BA Honors mid-80s to low-90s vs low-mid 70s general

name: University of Amsterdam
overall_acceptance_rate: Not officially published; secondary estimates vary (51% per EduRank, 4% per others)
test_policy: No SAT/ACT required; standardized tests not used
english_test_requirements: IELTS 6.5 overall (6.0 subscores); TOEFL iBT 92; or equivalent; exemptions for certain diplomas
application_pathways_eligibility: Studielink enrolment then UvA online app; international prior education assessed via diploma finder; EU/non-EU deadlines differ; refugees same process
deadline_calendar: No ED/EA; Numerus fixus: 15 Jan; Selective: 1 Feb; Non-selective intl: advisory 1 Apr (visa/housing), final 1 May
program_supplemental_requirements: CV/resume, motivation letter for some; selection tests for numerus fixus/ selective programs
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Transfers possible via Studielink for higher years if credits recognized; deferral not explicitly mentioned
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Varies by numerus fixus programs (e.g. Psychology, Business Admin); not published
acceptance_rate_by_residency: Not published; no in-state/OOS distinction; intl competitive due to quotas
admitted_gpa_percentiles: Not used; entry based on diploma equivalence to Dutch VWO
need_policy: Need-aware; merit-based scholarships, no need-blind admissions
class_profile_composition: ~43,000 students; ~36% international (15,632 intl); Bachelor's intake 7,259 (2025)
selectivity_trend: Intake decreasing (Bachelor's -6.5% 2025); total students down 1.9% after growth
tuition_fees: Statutory €2,601 (EU/EEA); Institutional €10,000-€37,500 non-EU by faculty/program (2025-26)
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Amsterdam Merit Scholarship (AMS) for non-EU: deadlines ~15 Jan; merit-based, GPA equiv 8.0+; faculty-specific
major_impaction_signals: Numerus fixus (fixed quota) for Business Admin, Economics, Psychology: 15 Jan deadline, selection
average_aid_merit_statistics: Not published; AMS ~€25,900 for select non-EU masters
deferral_waitlist_indicators: Numerus fixus: ranking list, places may open if others decline

name: University of Auckland
overall_acceptance_rate: Not officially published; secondary estimates 45-73%
test_policy: Not required (no SAT/ACT mentioned)
english_test_requirements: IELTS Academic 6.0 overall (no band <5.5); TOEFL iBT 80 (writing 21); equivalents incl. PTE 50, CAE 169
application_pathways_eligibility: Domestic: NCEA UE or equiv; International: country-specific quals/IB/CIE; Discretionary Entrance (under 20, NCEA L2+ exceptional); Special admission

admitted_gpa_percentiles: Uses NCEA rank score (varies by program, e.g. higher for competitive)

tuition_fees: Domestic undergrad 2026: ~NZ$7,600-8,800/yr (120pts); Int'l: NZ$38k-55k/yr (e.g. BA $40k, BSc $47-58k)
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Various scholarships worth $35M/yr; eligibility by merit/need; deadlines vary by award
major_impaction_signals: Competitive programs (e.g. Engineering, Commerce, Health) selected by rank score

name: University of Barcelona
overall_acceptance_rate: Approximately 69%
test_policy: Not applicable

application_pathways_eligibility: Spanish: PAU/EBAU, over 25/40/45, CFGS; Internationals: homologated foreign qualifications via UNEDassis

need_policy: Not applicable

name: University of Basel
overall_acceptance_rate: No official rate published; third-party estimates 19-28%
test_policy: No standardized tests (SAT/ACT) required; admission based on secondary school qualifications.
english_test_requirements: C1 recommended for English components; proof (B2/C1) only if required by faculty regs for specific programs. No universal TOEFL/IELTS.
application_pathways_eligibility: Swiss Maturität: eligible for all bachelors. Foreign: country-specific requirements, some need ECUS exam or proof of admission to equivalent program. Non-Swiss medicine/dental: additional numerus clausus.
deadline_calendar: No ED/EA/RD; Bachelor fall 2026: 30.04.2026; spring 2027: 30.11.2026. Master/Doc: similar. Medicine: 15.02.2026. Late apps possible in some cases.
program_supplemental_requirements: Medicine/dental: special provisions for non-Swiss. Language C1 German/English. Some programs faculty-specific.
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Recognition of foreign degrees for admission possible; no explicit deferred entry. Prior exclusions may bar admission.
program_specific_acceptance_rate: No rates published; medicine/dental limited by numerus clausus esp. for non-Swiss.
acceptance_rate_by_residency: No rates; non-Swiss restricted in medicine/dental.
admitted_gpa_percentiles: No data published; foreign grades converted via formula.
need_policy: Need-blind; admission qualification-based, low flat tuition for all.
class_profile_composition: ~13,000 students; ~25% international (~3,300).
tuition_fees: CHF 850 per semester for all enrolled students (Bachelor/Master); CHF 350 for Doctoral.
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Cantonal scholarships primary (parents' canton); uni supplementary for low-income in final phase (apply welfare office). Excellence scholarships for postgrad intl via gov.
acceptance_rate_by_round: No early/regular rounds; single deadline per semester.
major_impaction_signals: Medicine and dental medicine: numerus clausus, esp. non-Swiss.
average_aid_merit_statistics: No data; aid primarily need-based cantonal/uni funds (CHF 200-1000/semester).
deferral_waitlist_indicators: No waitlists; non-admission if unqualified. Late apps possible for some.

name: University of Bern
overall_acceptance_rate: Not officially published; aggregator estimates 19-25%
test_policy: Not required; no standardized tests like SAT/ACT mentioned
english_test_requirements: Program-specific for English-taught programs; e.g., TOEFL 92+, IELTS 6.0+ where required; otherwise self-assessed proficiency
application_pathways_eligibility: Swiss Matura: direct admission; International HS: country-specific or supplementary exam; Prior uni degree: eligible for advanced standing; Medical programs limited
deadline_calendar: Fall: Bachelor non-med April 30 (late Aug 31), Med Feb 15; Master April 30; No ED/EA
program_supplemental_requirements: Varies: CV, motivation letter, rec letters, interviews for select masters (e.g., AI Med, Biomed Eng); checklists per program
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Credit assessment by faculty (6-8 weeks); no explicit deferred entry policy noted
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Limited places in Medicine/Dental/Vet Med; others qualification-based, no rates published
acceptance_rate_by_residency: No data published; no in-state/OOS distinction
need_policy: Not specified; likely need-aware given public funding model
class_profile_composition: ~18,576 total enrollment; significant international presence
tuition_fees: Regular: CHF 850/semester from fall 2026; Non-Swiss: +CHF 1,700/semester; Semester fee CHF 34+
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Cantonal grants, SUB social fund; eligibility based on need/residency; no specific deadlines listed
major_impaction_signals: Numerus clausus for Medicine, Dental Medicine, Veterinary Medicine
deferral_waitlist_indicators: No waitlist or deferral data; late applications possible for some

name: University of Bristol
overall_acceptance_rate: 67% offer rate (62,228 applications, 41,592 offers) for 2024/25 cycle.
test_policy: No general standardized tests required. UCAT for Medicine/Dentistry; LNAT for Law.
english_test_requirements: Six profiles (A-F); IELTS 6.5-7.0 overall (no sub <6.0) typical for profile B/C; TOEFL iBT equivalent.
application_pathways_eligibility: UCAS for all UG (Home/Intl). Direct apply for Intl Foundation. VMCAS for Vet accelerated grad entry (North America).
deadline_calendar: UCAS: 15 Oct Med/Dent/Vet; 14 Jan equal consideration; 30 Jun Intl final (2026 entry). No ED/EA.
program_supplemental_requirements: UCAS personal statement, reference. Interviews/tests (UCAT/LNAT) for select programs.
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Transfers to Y2 possible if space/grades/content match. Deferred entry via dashboard.
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Business & Mgmt 8.9%; Med/Dent 9.6%; Engineering 10.7%; Law 18% (2025).
acceptance_rate_by_residency: Enrolled: 69% UK/Home, 27% Intl. Intl acceptance ~12% (2022); offer rates higher for Home.
need_policy: Need-aware for international (differential fees); Home via gov loans.
class_profile_composition: 56% female, 44% male; 69% UK, 27% Intl, 3% EU; 73% UG.
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Think Big UG: £6.5k/£13k, overseas status, apply by 10 Apr 2026 (open 21 Oct 2025); excl med/dent/vet.
major_impaction_signals: High demand: Business/Mgmt (9k apps), Engineering (8k), Medicine.
average_aid_merit_statistics: Home bursary up to £2,500 low-income; Think Big avg ~£8k merit intl.
deferral_waitlist_indicators: Deferral requestable. Apps held >8wks (waitlist-like).

name: University of California, Davis
overall_acceptance_rate: 44.6% for fall 2025 first-year class (102,980 applicants; 45,963 admits)
test_policy: Test blind: SAT/ACT scores no longer considered for admissions or scholarships
english_test_requirements: For international applicants where English is not primary language of instruction: TOEFL iBT (min 80 recommended), IELTS (min 7.0), Duolingo (min 115); exemptions if degree from English-only institution
application_pathways_eligibility: Freshman (post-secondary); Transfer (min GPA 2.80, CCC TAG for eligible CA CCC); Limited second bachelor's (specific majors from CCC); International same as domestic
deadline_calendar: Single application period Oct 1-Dec 1 (RD Nov 30 priority); no ED/EA; Transfer Nov 1-Jan (some Jan 15)
program_supplemental_requirements: None beyond UC application PIQs; all majors use standard UC app
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Transfer admission priority for CCC (TAG guarantee eligible); Deferred enrollment available post-admit
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Not publicly available; impacted majors (e.g. CS, engineering) lower than overall
acceptance_rate_by_residency: CA residents higher (~56% of admits); fall 2025 first-year: 25,677 CA admits of 45,963 total; OOS/US int'l lower due to caps
admitted_gpa_percentiles: High school GPA middle 50%: 4.00-4.26 (fall 2025)
need_policy: Admissions need-blind; aid need-aware for non-CA (limited state grants; federal available)
class_profile_composition: Fall 2025 admitted CA first-year: 4.0% Black, 0.6% AI/AN, 28.1% Hispanic, 41.5% Asian, 21.0% White; 34.6% first-gen
tuition_fees: 2025-26 cohort full-time annual: CA resident $17,346 (tuition $13,602 + fees); non-res $54,948 (+$37,602 NRST)
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: UC app auto-consider; campus scholarships Oct 1 app, min 3.25 GPA; FAFSA/CADAA Mar 2 priority; merit-based, some need
major_impaction_signals: High-demand/impacted: CS, engineering, bio sciences; recommend alternate major
average_aid_merit_statistics: 62% undergrads receive aid (avg $25,534); 53% CA cover tuition/fees; merit scholarships via app
deferral_waitlist_indicators: Deferral option available; waitlist used annually, outcomes vary

name: University of California, San Diego (UCSD)
overall_acceptance_rate: 28.4% (38,846/136,740 applicants) for fall 2025 freshman admits.
test_policy: Test-blind: SAT/ACT not considered in admissions or scholarships.
english_test_requirements: For non-native English speakers with non-English secondary instruction: TOEFL iBT 83, IELTS 7.0, Duolingo 115.
application_pathways_eligibility: UC Application for all (freshman/transfer/intl). Min GPA: CA res 3.0 (10-11th A-G), non-CA 3.4. ELC top 9% CA HS auto eligible.
deadline_calendar: Freshman: Nov 30; Transfer: Dec 4 (extended). No ED/EA; single RD deadline via UC app.
program_supplemental_requirements: 4 Personal Insight Questions (PIQs) required for all. Optional portfolio/audition for Arts majors (Music/Theatre/Visual Arts).
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Fall admission only. Min 60 semester/90 quarter UC-transferable units, GPA 3.0+. No explicit deferred entry mentioned; appeals rare.
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Limited data; e.g. transfer CS ~30% (2023). No official freshman major rates published.
admitted_gpa_percentiles: Middle 50% HS GPA (weighted, capped): 4.11-4.28.
need_policy: Need-based aid; unable to cover non-resident supplemental tuition (NRST) with grants, implying need-aware for non-residents.
class_profile_composition: Fall 2024 entering freshmen: Women ~52%, Asian 35%, Hispanic 26%, White 17%, Multiracial 6%, Intl 11%.
tuition_fees: 2024-25: In-state $14,934; Out-of-state $52,536 (incl. NRST).
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: FAFSA/CADAA March 2 priority. Merit (Regents/Chancellors) via UC app + need for some. CA res preferred for some.
major_impaction_signals: Selective majors (Biology, CS, Engineering impacted/capped); recommend alternate major.
deferral_waitlist_indicators: Waitlist opt-in by May 15; decisions by July 31; appeals rare; low success rate.

name: University of California, Santa Barbara (UCSB)
overall_acceptance_rate: 38% (fall 2025, Class of 2029: 110,184 applicants, ~41,870 admits)
test_policy: Test-free (SAT/ACT not considered in admissions or scholarships)
english_test_requirements: TOEFL, IELTS, or DET required for non-native English speakers unless waived (e.g., English-medium instruction); official scores due March 15
application_pathways_eligibility: Freshman (HS seniors/recent grads); Transfer (CCC priority, junior-level); International; TAG for CCC to select L&S pre-majors (excl. performing arts/Eng/CCS)
deadline_calendar: UC Application: Opens Aug 1, Submit Oct 1-Nov 30/Dec 2 (fall only); No ED/EA; TAG Sep 1-30
program_supplemental_requirements: CCS: supplemental app reviewed by faculty; Dance/Music B.M./B.F.A./B.A.: auditions; Engineering: no alternate majors; other L&S generally none
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Transfers: junior-level priority from CCCs, TAG for select majors; Deferred entry: not standard (case-by-case via FAQ); max 70 LD units
acceptance_rate_by_residency: In-state: 32.1%; OOS: 54.78%; International: 48.14% (Class of 2029)
admitted_gpa_percentiles: Enrolled: 90%+ GPA 4.0; mid-50% 4.10-4.29 weighted; avg UC GPA 4.18
need_policy: Need-based financial aid; likely need-blind for CA residents, need-aware for non-residents (UC standard)
class_profile_composition: Undergrad 23,181; Hispanic/Latino 28%, White 36%, Asian 26%, Black 5% est.; Female 56%; Int'l ~9%
selectivity_trend: 2029:38%, 2028:33%, 2027:28%, 2026:26%; apps stable ~110k, rates down then up slightly
tuition_fees: In-state tuition/fees: $16,929; Non-res supp: +$37,602; Total COA: $47,418 / $85,020 (incl room/board)
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Need-based UCSB scholarships for CA residents; FAFSA/CADAA priority deadline March 2; via UC app scholarships section
major_impaction_signals: Selective/impacted: All Engineering/CS (no alts), CCS (supp app), L&S Bio/Econ/Math/Physics (prep GPA reqs 2.7-3.4), performing arts auditions
average_aid_merit_statistics: Avg need-based scholarship/grant: $27,000; Total need-based: $283M; Non-need: $23M (no merit specified)

name: University of Cambridge
overall_acceptance_rate: 16.4% (3,632 acceptances out of 22,153 applications in 2024 admissions cycle)
test_policy: No SAT/ACT required. Course-specific pre-interview admissions tests required for many programs (e.g. ESAT for Engineering, Natural Sciences; TMUA for Computer Science, Economics; LNAT for Law; UCAT for Medicine). College-specific at-interview assessments for some arts/humanities.
english_test_requirements: IELTS Academic: 7.5 overall (7.0+ each); TOEFL iBT/Home (pre-Jan 2026): 110 overall (25+ each); Cambridge C2 Proficiency: 200 (185+ no element); or equivalent C1 CEFR from school exam.
application_pathways_eligibility: All undergraduate applicants apply via UCAS. Same process for UK/Home, EU, International, mature (21+), disabled, care leavers. Mature may have January UCAS deadline but Cambridge requires October deadline for consideration.
deadline_calendar: Single deadline: UCAS application by 15 October 2026 (6pm UK time) for 2027 entry. My Cambridge Application by 22 October 2026. No ED/EA/RD rounds.
program_supplemental_requirements: My Cambridge Application (post-UCAS): funding evidence for international students, optional Cambridge-specific personal statement, £60 application fee for overseas applicants. Written work submission for some courses (e.g. History, English).
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Deferred entry: possible, specify in UCAS. Transfers: possible from other UK universities but rare and course/college-specific; not from overseas. Check course pages.
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Varies: Architecture 12.2% (64/526), Veterinary Medicine 19.9% (67/336), Natural Sciences ~14%; full table in 2024 cycle report (22k apps total).
acceptance_rate_by_residency: Not explicitly stated; Home (UK) schools: Maintained 18.8%, Independent 11.1%; Overseas implied lower (~10-12% from secondary sources). No in-state/OOS (UK national).
need_policy: Not explicitly stated; likely need-aware for international students (must provide funding evidence); Home students government loans.
class_profile_composition: 48% men:52% women; 15.8% declared disability; 94.7% A*AA or better (best 3 A-levels); from maintained schools 52.2% of acceptances.
tuition_fees: Home (first UG): £9,535/year; International: £29,052 (Group 1 arts/humanities) to £50,000+ (Medicine/Vet) + College fee £10,017-£13,662 (2025-26 rates, fixed for course duration).
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Cambridge Trust (int'l, post-offer); college bursaries; eligibility: academic merit/need; deadlines vary (often Jan-May post-offer). Full funding evidence required by May/June for visa.
major_impaction_signals: High competition: Medicine, Computer Science, Economics, HSPS (success rates <15%); ~6 apps/place overall.
deferral_waitlist_indicators: Winter Pool: ~1,000 applicants pooled, ~200 offers (~20% success); August pool for unmet conditions.

name: University of Cape Town
overall_acceptance_rate: Approximately 29% for undergraduate (17,903 admissions out of 61,903 applicants in 2023; trend downward from 46% in 2019)
test_policy: Required: National Benchmark Tests (NBTs) for all SA resident/schooling undergrad applicants and all Health Sciences applicants
english_test_requirements: For non-English medium intl: TOEFL iBT 88+ (L19 W19 R20 S22), IELTS 6.5+ (no band <6.0), PTE 67+ (skills 59+), NBT AL upper intermediate+
application_pathways_eligibility: SA NSC bachelor's endorsement + FPS/WPS; intl quals w/ USAF exemption; redress for disadvantaged SA (DF>1, race/socioeconomic weighting); first-time undergrad
deadline_calendar: Single RD deadline 31 July 2026 for 2027 UG; apps open 1 Apr; NBTs by 3 Oct 2026; intl supporting docs 30 Sep/31 Oct/15 Nov/31 Dec
program_supplemental_requirements: Portfolios for Fine Art/Arch; NBTs for Health/Commerce/Law/Eng; no essays/supps for most
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Transfers: submit transcripts by 31 Oct; deferred entry possible, program change by 31 Aug
acceptance_rate_by_residency: 16% international students; no split rates (mostly SA applicants ~95%+
admitted_gpa_percentiles: NSC-based FPS min 550+ (e.g. Science 550-720+); no US GPA
need_policy: Need-aware; redress for disadvantaged; NSFAS for low-income (<R350k household)
class_profile_composition: 2023 UG enrolments: 45% Black SA (African/Coloured/Indian); 28.9% first-time NSFAS; intl ~16%
selectivity_trend: Applicants up 63% (37k 2019 to 62k 2023); accept rate down from 46% to 29%
tuition_fees: Course-based; local UG first payment R34.5k; intl R121k+; full varies R60-80k ZAR/yr typical
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: NSFAS apply via nsfas.org.za (opens ~Sep); household <R350k; UCT entrance schols auto for high FPS/NBT; Gap funding for missing middle
average_aid_merit_statistics: NSFAS covers full for eligible; UCT top-up bursaries; entrance schols R20-40k

name: University of Chicago
overall_acceptance_rate: 4.48% for Class of 2028 (43,612 applications, 1,955 admits); no data yet for Class of 2030
test_policy: Test-optional with 'no harm' policy: scores considered only if they strengthen application
english_test_requirements: TOEFL iBT or IELTS Academic required for non-native speakers unless waived (e.g., English-medium education); undergrad specifics align with grad min scores ~100 TOEFL/7.0 IELTS
application_pathways_eligibility: ED I (binding, Nov 1), EA (non-binding, Nov 1), ED II (binding, Jan 6), RD (Jan 6); open to all first-year applicants including international; transfer separate
deadline_calendar: ED I/EA: Nov 1, 2025; ED II/RD: Jan 6, 2026; decisions mid-Dec EA/ED1, early Feb ED2, late March RD
program_supplemental_requirements: UChicago supplemental essays: one 'Why UChicago?' (300-600 words), one extended essay (500-700 words) from prompts; optional arts supplements
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Transfers: TED Feb 15, Rolling Mar 1-Jun; must complete 2 years at UChicago. Deferred/gap year: considered case-by-case before Jun 15 for admitted students
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Single undergraduate College; no program-specific rates published
acceptance_rate_by_residency: Private university; no in-state preference. Intl ~18% of class
need_policy: Need-blind for domestic; need-aware for international (aid eligibility set at admission)
class_profile_composition: Intl 18%, Hispanic 17%, Asian 19%, Black 7%, White 29%, Multirace 7% (approx from CDS undergrad)
tuition_fees: $71,325 tuition (2025-26); total COA ~$99k
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Auto-considered merit scholarships; need-based same as admission deadlines; Odyssey for low-income/first-gen
acceptance_rate_by_round: Not publicly released; ED typically higher (TBA for 2030)
major_impaction_signals: No major impaction; open declaration
average_aid_merit_statistics: Avg need-based grant $67,505 (first-year)
deferral_waitlist_indicators: Waitlist not ranked; deferral requests case-by-case; no specific stats

name: University of Copenhagen
overall_acceptance_rate: No official overall acceptance rate published; unofficial estimate ~37%; program-specific varies (e.g. MSc Computer Science 192/631 ~30% in 2025)
test_policy: No SAT/ACT required or accepted; Bachelor's Quota 2 requires Danish-language test and interview
english_test_requirements: Bachelor's: Danish proficiency (Studieprøven i dansk); Master's: English B level equivalent (TOEFL iBT 83+, IELTS 6.5+, etc. per program)
application_pathways_eligibility: Bachelor's: Quota 1 (GPA>=6.0 Danish, EU/EEA/IB); Quota 2 (test/interview, all incl. non-EU); Master's: Direct app via portal, EU/EEA vs non-EU deadlines
deadline_calendar: Bachelor's: Quota 2 15 Mar, Quota 1 5 Jul; Master's non-EU: 15 Jan/1 Mar, EU: 15 Oct
program_supplemental_requirements: Bachelor's: Specific subject levels (A/B/C Danish equiv.); Master's: Vary by program (e.g. CV, motivation for some)
transfer_deferred_entry_options: No specific info found; credit transfer possible via ECTS
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Varies; e.g. MSc Economics (DK) 331 admitted (high applicants); MSc Computer Science 192/631 ~30%; see stats
acceptance_rate_by_residency: Master's: Varies by program (e.g. 98% DK vs 2% int'l in some); no undergrad split
admitted_gpa_percentiles: Quota 1 min Danish GPA 6.0; no percentiles published
need_policy: N/A - Public university; merit-based scholarships

major_impaction_signals: Capped intakes for many programs; quota 2 ~10% places
average_aid_merit_statistics: No data; scholarships cover tuition +/- living for select non-EU
deferral_waitlist_indicators: No waitlist/deferral info; annual intake September

name: University of Edinburgh
overall_acceptance_rate: Offer rate 53% (2025 cycle: 36,195 offers from 68,862 applications); enrollment rate ~11% (7,626 acceptances)
test_policy: Test-blind for SAT/ACT; no standardized tests required for most programmes; UCAT required for Medicine
english_test_requirements: IELTS 6.5 overall (min 5.5-6.0 components) or equivalent (TOEFL-iBT 92, etc.); school qualifications (e.g. GCSE C/4, IB SL 5); varies slightly by degree; max 3.5 years old
application_pathways_eligibility: UCAS application for all UG; Widening access contextual minimum offers for eligible UK residents (postcode SIMD/POLAR/ACORN, school, care-experienced, SWAP etc.); standard offers otherwise
deadline_calendar: UCAS 2026 entry: opens 1 Sep 2025; Medicine/Vet 15 Oct 2025; equal consideration 14 Jan 2026; some programs close earlier/later

transfer_deferred_entry_options: Deferred entry via UCAS choice; advanced entry Year 2/3 possible if qualifications meet higher grades (e.g. A*AA Advanced Highers); no standard credit transfer policy stated
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Varies widely by selection area/fee status; e.g. Medicine ~10-20% offer rate; Business Studies low for Scotland (~18%); Economics ~40-60%; full tables in PDFs
acceptance_rate_by_residency: By fee status (Scotland restricted/high comp; RUK/ROI; Overseas); offer rates vary e.g. Overseas Economics 60%, Medicine low; separate PDFs

tuition_fees: Scotland: £1,820/yr; RUK/ROI: £9,790 (2026-27); International: fixed by program/start e.g. £26,000 Arts/Sci, up to £54,560 Medicine
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: RUK Edinburgh Scholarship: auto via SLC means-test (income <£42k); International: various merit/need, some deadlines March; SAAS for Scots

name: University of Erlangen-Nürnberg
overall_acceptance_rate: Approximately 40-50% (aggregator estimates); 90.7% of programs NC-free (open if qualified), 9.3% restricted.
test_policy: Not applicable; no SAT/ACT or similar standardized tests required. Language proficiency tests mandatory.
english_test_requirements: TOEFL iBT 95+ or IELTS 6.5+ for English-taught Masters; varies by program (e.g. B2/C1 level).
application_pathways_eligibility: campo.fau.de for most; hochschulstart.de for Medicine/Dentistry/Pharmacy; non-EU pay 100EUR fee from WS2026/27; Bildungsinländer/EU separate; APS for China/India/Vietnam.

transfer_deferred_entry_options: Higher semester applications possible if places; reapply next semester if not enrolled without fee if same year/program.
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Restricted programs use NC (grade avg): e.g. Psychology BSc 1.6, Molecular Medicine 1.7; others open.
need_policy: Not applicable; public university admissions based on qualifications only, no financial consideration.
class_profile_composition: WS2025/26: 38,625 total students, 9,774 international (25%), ~50% female, 4,216 first-time.
selectivity_trend: Stable enrollment ~38-40k; slight decrease WS2025/26 due to Abitur changes.
tuition_fees: No tuition fees; semester contribution €72-€150 (includes public transport); non-EU app fee €100 from WS2026/27.

name: University of Geneva
overall_acceptance_rate: No official rate published; secondary sources estimate 20-26%
test_policy: No SAT/ACT required. ECUS exam required for many foreign secondary diplomas not equivalent to Swiss Maturité.

application_pathways_eligibility: Swiss Maturité: direct. Recognized foreign diplomas: specific grade averages + ECUS for many countries. Non-equivalent: prior recognized Bachelor's or 2 years uni + ECUS waiver possible.
deadline_calendar: Fall: Jan 15-Feb 28 (visa/ECUS needed) or Apr 30. Spring: Sep 15-30 (visa) or Nov 30. No ED/EA.
program_supplemental_requirements: Varies; e.g., GSEM: French language test B2+. Specific faculties may have additional conditions.
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Transfer: Equivalence validation for prior studies at application. Deferred entry: not explicitly mentioned; possible via leave of absence post-admission.
acceptance_rate_by_residency: No splits published. Deadlines differ by visa need (international proxy).
admitted_gpa_percentiles: Varies by country; e.g., 70% average for some diplomas.
need_policy: Not specified; public university with low flat tuition for all.
class_profile_composition: Enrollment ~17,744 total; international mix but no detailed demographics.
tuition_fees: CHF 500 per semester (all students, nationalities).
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Limited for regular students; exchange scholarships available. Cantonal aid possible. Deadlines vary.
acceptance_rate_by_round: No early/regular rounds; single deadlines by profile.

application_pathways_eligibility: UCAS for Home/Scotland/RUK/EU; Common App for International (non-EU/UK); fee status determines
deadline_calendar: UCAS: 14 Jan 2026 (equal consideration); Common App rolling to 30 Jun 2026; no ED/EA
program_supplemental_requirements: Interviews/tests for Medicine, Dentistry, Vet Med, some others (e.g. auditions); personal statement via UCAS/Common App
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Deferred entry possible except Dentistry/Nursing/Teacher/Med/Vet; transfers welcomed but prior degree no guarantee; adult returners supported
need_policy: Need-aware (merit-based scholarships; home students SAAS loans)
class_profile_composition: 60% female, 39% male; 62% UK, 5% EU, 33% int'l; 85% full-time; 60% UG

tuition_fees: Scotland: £1,820 (capped); RUK: £9,535; Int'l/EU: Arts £26,580, Sci/Eng £31,800, Med £58,890 etc. (2025/26)
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Automatic/merit/WP-based (e.g. World Changers Int'l £7-10k/yr A*AA+; Talent £1.5k Scottish WP; RUK Access income-based); apply post-offer
major_impaction_signals: Competitive: Medicine/Dentistry/Vet/Law; adjusted offers for WP

name: University of Göttingen

transfer_deferred_entry_options: Higher subject semesters: deadlines 1Jun-15Jul winter, 1Dec-15Jan summer (restricted); change of university/ program supported
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Not published as %; NC cutoffs e.g. Medicine WiSe2025/26: waiting 7 sem (2.4), qual 12.29 pts
need_policy: Need-aware (inferred)
class_profile_composition: 31,619 total; ~13% international (~4,500 from 138 countries)

scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: DAAD, faculty-specific (e.g. law tuition waivers auto-considered); no unified uni deadlines; intl scholarship holders reduced fees
major_impaction_signals: 29.9% programs admission-restricted (NC/selection); medicine, law, business, psych high demand
deferral_waitlist_indicators: Nachrückverfahren (subsequent allocation); waiting lists per program; limited deferral

name: University of Hamburg
test_policy: Not applicable

program_supplemental_requirements: Language proficiency (German C1 for most); foreign credential recognition; some programs English B2; program-specific docs
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Transfers to higher semesters if places available; deferred admission for service/childcare/military via hochschulstart.de
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Varies; e.g. Informatik NC 2.5 (grade), 8 WS (2021); Politikwissenschaft 2.6, 4 WS; Medizin ~1.0-2.0; latest NC PDFs on site
acceptance_rate_by_residency: Separate quotas: non-EU bonus system; EU via national NC
need_policy: Not applicable; tuition-free public uni; merit-based scholarships

tuition_fees: No tuition fees; semester contribution ~350 EUR incl. transport pass
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Merit scholarships for internationals: 15 Apr/15 Oct; enrolled 2+ sem, good grades, no BAföG eligible
major_impaction_signals: NC for restricted programs indicates demand > capacity
deferral_waitlist_indicators: Succession procedure after main; waiting lists via NC/waiting semesters

name: University of Helsinki
overall_acceptance_rate: No university-wide rate; international master's programs: 34% (1416 admitted / 4175 applicants) for autumn 2025 intake.
test_policy: Test-blind for international master's; SAT/ACT required for Bachelor's in Science Admission Group 1 (applicants without Finnish/IB/EB/RP/DIA); certificate-based for Group 2.
english_test_requirements: IELTS Academic 6.5 (min 6.0 writing); TOEFL iBT 92; PTE Academic 62 (min 54 each); CAE/CPE 176 (no <162); or English-medium degree/upper secondary from approved countries; YKI level 5 all skills.
application_pathways_eligibility: International Master's: bachelor's degree (or equiv) + sufficient applicable studies + English proficiency; Bachelor's Science Group 1: high school diploma + SAT/ACT; Group 2: Finnish/IB/EB/RP/DIA grades; Open University path available.
deadline_calendar: Single annual autumn intake; Intl Master's & Bach Group 1: 2-16 Jan; Bach Group 2: 10-24 Mar (2026); results Apr-Jul; accept offer by Jul.
program_supplemental_requirements: Master's: application form, transcripts, degree cert, motivation/CV/refs (program-specific); Bachelor's: SAT/ACT direct from provider, passport copy, language proof.
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Credit transfer for prior formal/non-formal learning possible via application; restrictions on duplicate degrees/study rights; conditional admission for pending degrees (meet by Aug/Sep); no dedicated transfer admission.
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Varies by program; e.g. Computer Science: 578 apps, intake 40; Data Science: 545 apps, intake 30 (2025); full list in stats PDFs; no exact admit rates.
acceptance_rate_by_residency: 65% applications non-EU/EEA; admitted intl masters: 79% non-Finnish, 55% non-EU/EEA (2025); no split rates.
admitted_sat_act_percentiles: Bachelor's Science Group 1 lowest accepted SAT: 1480 (2025), 1490 (2024); no percentiles or ACT specifics.
need_policy: Need-aware/merit-based; scholarships merit-only (academic excellence); no need-based aid; tuition fixed by nationality.
class_profile_composition: Intl masters 2025: 1416 admitted from 83 countries; 21% Finnish, 79% international (55% non-EU/EEA); top origins: China, Pakistan, Bangladesh, US.
selectivity_trend: Intl masters apps dropped 60% in 2025 (4175 vs prior ~10k) due to new app fee; higher eligible proportion; Bachelor's stable ~500 apps.
tuition_fees: 13,000-18,000 EUR/academic year for non-EU/EEA (program-specific); free for EU/EEA/Swiss/permanent residents.
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Apply simultaneously with admission (Jan); non-EU/EEA tuition-liable; merit-based (prior academic merit); 50%/100% waivers; renew w/ 55 ECTS/yr; limited numbers.
major_impaction_signals: All programs have fixed intakes (e.g. CS 40, Data Sci 30); popular: CS/Data Sci >500 apps each; overbooking used.
deferral_waitlist_indicators: Reserve lists used for some programs; conditional admits common (submit docs by May/Aug); no deferral policy mentioned.

name: University of Indonesia
overall_acceptance_rate: Approximately 8% (estimated from ratios)
test_policy: Varies by pathway: SNBP (prestasi/grades, no test), SNBT (UTBK required), SIMAK (UI entrance test required), International (application + proficiency tests)
english_test_requirements: International: TOEFL ITP 500+, iBT 61-94, IELTS 5.5-7.0, EPT UI 500+ depending on program/faculty; BIPA Indonesian cert for some
application_pathways_eligibility: SNBP: eligible HS seniors based on prestasi/rapor; SNBT: all via national UTBK; SIMAK UI: recent HS grads (limits on year); Talentscouting: olympiad/prestasi winners; PPKB: mandiri; International: non-Indonesian citizens
deadline_calendar: SNBP: reg 3-18 Feb 2026, ann 31 Mar; SNBT: UTBK reg Mar-Apr, test 21-30 Apr, ann 25 May; SIMAK S1: Mar-Apr/Jun 2026; International Phase1:19 Feb-27 Mar, Phase2:1 May-9 Jun (2025 cycle); multiple phases per jalur
program_supplemental_requirements: Intl Medicine: iBT TOEFL 94+/IELTS 7+, BIPA lvl3, MMPI; Computer Sci/Comm: TOEFL 550+/IELTS 5.5+; health certs; faculty-specific
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Transfer credit: yes (up to 50% for exchanges/MOOC/PJJ, faculty approved); Deferred entry: no info found
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Varies widely; e.g. Medicine SNBP 2025 keketatan ~20-50:1, CS/Engineering 10-30:1; full table at simak.ui.ac.id/keketatan
acceptance_rate_by_residency: Separate intl quotas (small, e.g. Medicine intl 20 slots); locals via national/mandiri; intl compete separately but low volume
need_policy: Need-aware: UKT tiers 1-11 based on family income/assets
tuition_fees: UKT IDR 0.5M-20M/semester (tier 1-11 by income/prog); Mandiri + IPI IDR 25M-125M+ one-time; intl higher
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Multiple via beasiswa.ui.ac.id (KIP Kuliah, BI, JNE, etc.); deadlines vary by program (e.g. Sep for some); prestasi/need-based
major_impaction_signals: High impaction: Medicine, Dentistry, CS, Engineering (keketatan 20+:1); quotas listed

name: University of Lausanne

name: University of Maastricht

deferral_waitlist_indicators: Deferrals allowed; no waitlists used

transfer_deferred_entry_options: Credit/RPL assessed on admission application for prior studies/experience; non-award/cross-inst study credit possible; deferral options via UAC/direct
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Not publicly available
acceptance_rate_by_residency: Not publicly available
admitted_gpa_percentiles: Not published; domestic uses ATAR/selection rank (medians vary by program)
admitted_sat_act_percentiles: N/A - No SAT/ACT required or reported

name: University of Oslo
test_policy: Test-blind: No standardized tests like SAT/ACT required. Admission based on prior degree GPA.
english_test_requirements: Master's: TOEFL iBT 90 (19R/20L/20S/24W min); IELTS Academic 6.5 (6.5 each); PTE 62 (58 each). Exemptions for prior English education. Varies slightly by program.
application_pathways_eligibility: Separate quotas/deadlines: Non-EU/EEA/Swiss (Dec 1, up to 2 programs); EU/EEA/Swiss (Mar 1, up to 3); Norway/Nordic residents (Apr 15, up to 3). Norwegian bachelor's require Norwegian proficiency.
deadline_calendar: Master's Aug start: Non-EU Dec 1 (results Apr 5); EU Mar 1 (Apr 30); Norway/Nordic Apr 15 (Jul 7). Jan start: Oct 15. No ED/EA; priority-based.
program_supplemental_requirements: Yes; e.g. Data Science: 80 ECTS specific math/programming/stats courses, avg grade Norwegian C (~US B). Course descriptions required if external.
admitted_gpa_percentiles: Min avg grade Norwegian C (~US B) in 80 ECTS specialization; ranked by GPA.
class_profile_composition: Total enrollment ~26,650; ~30% undergrad, 70% grad (secondary).
selectivity_trend: Slight increase in master's applicants/offers 2025 vs 2024 (10,755 appl, 5,363 offers vs prior).
tuition_fees: Non-EU/EEA/Swiss: 198,000-286,000 NOK/year master's (2025/26); EU/EEA/Norwegian: free + semester fee ~590 NOK.
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: No scholarships offered by UiO for full bachelor's/master's degree programs.
major_impaction_signals: Limited places e.g. 20 for Data Science; high competition in Informatics (1354 first-choice appl).
deferral_waitlist_indicators: Supplementary admissions from waiting lists Jul 14, Aug 5,18 if places available.

deadline_calendar: UCAS: 15 Oct 2025 6pm UK; Tests: 21-27 Oct; Written work: 10 Nov; Interviews: early-mid Dec; Decisions: 13-14 Jan 2026. Single deadline, no ED/EA/RD
program_supplemental_requirements: Course-specific: admissions tests (~50 courses), written work (humanities/languages), portfolio (Art); see table per course
transfer_deferred_entry_options: No transfers (start year 1); Deferred entry accepted except Fine Art, History of Art, Grad Medicine; competitive
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Varies widely: Economics & Mgmt 5.5%, Comp Sci 6.4%, Maths & Phil 6.9%, Biomed Sci 8.7%, Medicine ~9% (2024/25)
need_policy: Need-aware, esp. international; aid assessed post-admission via household income
class_profile_composition: 3,245 admitted (2024); 80.5% UK; 85.6% A*AA+ A-levels; gender/ethnicity/SES breakdowns in report
tuition_fees: Home/UK: £9,790 (2026/27); Overseas: £37,380-£62,820 (varies by course)
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Crankstart (UK ≤£32,500 income, auto); Reach Oxford (low-income countries, app 4 Feb 2026); college-specific
